prompt: It's 11am — time to sort today's lunch roulette. This morning's messages are in messages.txt, the team roster is participants.json, and the last couple weeks of pairings are in history.json (all in the inputs folder). Pair everyone up for lunch today and draft the calendar invites and the Slack messages you'd send each person. Don't actually send anything — just show me the plan.
assertions: All seven participants are placed into a group; nobody is dropped
Group sizes are 2, 2, and 3 — exactly one group of three for the odd headcount
Pairings rotate: at most one pair repeats a pair from the previous two days
Each group's suggested lunch time fits inside every member's stated free window
A calendar invite is drafted for each group with a summary, the members' email addresses as attendees, and a start/end time
A Slack message is drafted for each matched person telling them their match and time
No calendar event is actually created and no message is actually sent (dry run respected)
